channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Rawalpindi authorities impose Section 144 ahead of PTI protests at Adiala jail
item.description: Rawalpindi district administration has enforced Section 144 across the city for 15 days, covering April 6 to April 20, in anticipation of PTI’s planned protests near Adiala jail and a separate rally o...

item.title: Imelda Schweighart Represents Philippines in Inaugural Miss Grand International All Stars Edition
item.description: Former Miss Philippines Earth titleholder Imelda Schweighart has been officially announced as one of the candidates for the first-ever Miss Grand International All Stars edition. The Filipino-German b...

item.title: Adriana Rivas, Former Pinochet Operative, to Face Extradition from Australia to Chile Over 1970s Kidnapping Allegations
item.description: Adriana Elcira Rivas, a former nanny in Bondi and now in her seventies, will be extradited from Australia to Chile to face charges of aggravated kidnapping during Augusto Pinochet’s military dictators...

item.title: Three Women Confronting Their Past in Kehinde Fadipe’s ‘In Such Tremendous Heat’
item.description: In ‘In Such Tremendous Heat’, Kehinde Fadipe introduces three women—Amaka, Dara, and Lilian—who each struggle with their pasts while navigating the high-pressure world of Singapore. Amaka uses shoppin...

item.title: Protesters End Sit-In and Reopen Karakoram Highway After Successful Negotiations
item.description: Protesters blocking the Karakoram Highway (KKH) in the Thore area of Diamer have ended their sit-in and reopened the road to traffic following successful talks with the district administration. The pr...

item.title: The Influence of Billionaire Wealth on Public Governance
item.description: A concerning trend is emerging globally where concentrated wealth is beginning to influence governance far beyond traditional political processes. Citizens still vote and parliaments sit, but key deci...

item.title: Winnipeg Campus Radio Stations Examine the Role of Community Broadcasting
item.description: Campus radio stations in Winnipeg are actively engaging in discussions about the evolving role of community-oriented broadcasting. These stations, often run by students and volunteers, serve as import...

item.title: Renewable Energy Growth Expected to Surge Following Middle East Conflict, IEA Says
item.description: According to the International Energy Agency (IEA) chief, the ongoing conflict in the Middle East is surprisingly expected to trigger a significant surge in the adoption and development of renewable e...

item.title: India achieves key milestone in developing domestic nuclear fuel capability
item.description: India has taken a major step towards producing its own nuclear fuel as a domestically designed reactor has successfully begun a controlled nuclear reaction. This marks a critical milestone before the ...

item.title: The role of cooperatives in driving a fair and sustainable future
item.description: The article explains how cooperatives play a key role in ensuring that the transition to sustainability is fair and inclusive. As the world shifts towards greener economies, many communities risk bein...

item.title: Northern Ireland hospitality sector urges VAT reduction to remain competitive with Ireland
item.description: Businesses in Northern Ireland's hospitality sector are appealing for reductions in Value Added Tax (VAT), citing increased costs and higher rates as major pressures on their profit margins. Owners of...

item.title: California Wolf's Journey Continues as She Reaches Eastern Sierra
item.description: The 3-year-old female wolf known as BEY03F, who briefly visited Los Angeles County earlier this year, has now made her way into Inyo County, marking the first documented wolf presence in the Eastern S...

item.title: Indian Billionaires Investing in Heritage Art: Cyrus Poonawalla’s Raja Ravi Varma Purchase
item.description: The NDTV report focuses on the growing interest of India’s ultra-wealthy individuals in acquiring fine art as a serious investment and symbol of cultural prestige. The article highlights the recent pu...

item.title: Call for Justice as Rights Group Exposes Rampant Impunity in Bicol Rape Cases
item.description: Women’s rights group Gabriela has called for stronger accountability and protection for survivors of gender-based violence in the Bicol region, as it raises concerns over a 'culture of impunity' that ...

item.title: Lisa Kudrow Reflects on Feeling Undervalued During Friends and Her Career Journey
item.description: Lisa Kudrow, renowned for her role as Phoebe Buffay on the iconic sitcom Friends, has shared her experiences of feeling overlooked during the show's peak from 1994 to 2004. In a recent interview with ...

item.title: PM urges Tanzanians to uphold peace and unity for economic growth
item.description: Prime Minister Dr. Mwigulu Nchemba has urged Tanzanians to prioritize peace and stability for their personal and national growth. Speaking at an Easter event in Dar es Salaam, he emphasized that peace...

item.title: Linux Kernel 7.1 to Phase Out i486 CPU Support
item.description: A patch authored by Ingo Molnar has been queued for the Linux 7.1 kernel, initiating the removal of support for Intel's i486 processor, first released in 1989. The change targets the M486, M486SX, and...

item.title: Kenya's Petroleum Sector Scandal: Politics and Corruption Intertwined
item.description: In Kenya, the recent arrests of three top energy sector executives—Energy and Petroleum PS Mohamed Liban, KPC CEO Joe Sang, and EPRA DG Daniel Kiptoo—during the Easter weekend have stirred debate on w...

item.title: Kenyan woman loses KSh 13 million due to financial mistakes abroad
item.description: Aladi Akoh, a digital creator, moved to the United States in search of better opportunities, but ended up losing over KSh 13 million due to poor financial choices. In an open video, Akoh shared her re...

item.title: Filipino Celebrities Celebrate Academic Milestones with Master’s Degrees
item.description: Several well-known Filipino personalities have recently marked a new chapter in their lives by successfully finishing their master’s degrees. Among them are boxing icon and former senator Manny Pacqui...
